title: The Evolution of Dialects
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Language is not a (0) static entity; it is constantly evolving. The (17) of certain dialects can be traced back to historical migrations. When two groups interact, a (18) of linguistic features often occurs, leading to the (19) of new hybrid forms. || This process is frequently (20) by those who wish to preserve 'pure' language. They view such changes as a (21) of cultural identity. However, linguists argue that the (22) of a language lies in its ability to adapt. To (23) these complex shifts, one must look at the (24) between social status and speech patterns.
